rule_schema.standard_reference: Exact section or rule reference in standards/adaptive-layouts.md.
rule_schema.section: Human section name from the standard.
rule_schema.title: Short operational title.
rule_schema.requirement: Normative requirement extracted from the standard.
rule_schema.applies_to: Repo surfaces where the rule applies.
rule_schema.trigger_kinds: Change categories that should pull this rule into an applicable checklist.
rule_schema.phases: Workflow phases where this rule is relevant.
rule_schema.enforcement_mode: validator-required, checklist-required, checklist-and-validator, advisory, or backlog.
rule_schema.evidence_required: What an implementation or verification receipt should record when applicable.
rule_schema.automation: Current or intended mechanical enforcement owner.

rules.title: Design one adaptive product experience
rules.requirement: Design one product experience that adapts across desktop, tablet, Android, and iOS.
evidence_required: UI plans and receipts describe one adaptive product experience rather than separate unrelated desktop and mobile flows.
Rendered verification covers desktop, tablet, and mobile-relevant states when UI layout changes.

rules.title: Define reusable layout modules before feature expansion
rules.requirement: Every app should define reusable layout modules before feature work expands.
evidence_required: Feature expansion either uses existing reusable layout modules or records why a module is not applicable.
New app templates include the relevant reusable layout primitives for expected app surfaces.

rules.title: Provide top-level app shell and navigation shell
rules.requirement: Apps define AppShell for top-level frame, theme, providers, and route outlet, and NavigationShell for desktop sidebar plus mobile tabs or drawer.
evidence_required: App frame, providers, and route outlet are handled through a top-level shell where applicable.
Navigation adapts from desktop sidebar to a mobile-appropriate tab or drawer pattern.

rules.title: Use page list-detail form table and settings layout primitives
rules.requirement: Apps define PageHeader, ListDetailLayout, FormLayout, DataTableLayout, and SettingsLayout where those surfaces are present.
evidence_required: Pages with titles, actions, breadcrumbs, or status use a PageHeader or equivalent established primitive.
List/detail, form, table, and settings surfaces use matching layout primitives when present.

rules.title: Prefer component-driven layout and container queries
rules.requirement: Use component-driven layout where possible. Prefer container queries for reusable modules and viewport breakpoints for app shell decisions.
evidence_required: Reusable modules use component-driven layout or container-aware behavior where practical.
Viewport breakpoints are reserved for app shell decisions where component context is insufficient.

rules.title: Verify required responsive widths
rules.requirement: Minimum required layout checks cover desktop at 1440px wide, laptop at 1280px wide, tablet at 768px wide, and mobile at 390px wide.
evidence_required: Rendered verification records checks at 1440px, 1280px, 768px, and 390px widths when layout changes.
Skipped viewport checks include an explicit blocker or scope reason.

rules.title: Prevent overlap clipping and mobile overflow
rules.requirement: Adaptive layouts have no overlapping text or controls, no horizontal overflow on mobile, and no clipped text or controls.
evidence_required: Rendered verification confirms text and controls do not overlap or clip.
Mobile verification confirms no horizontal overflow.

rules.title: Keep primary actions reachable on mobile
rules.requirement: Primary actions remain reachable on mobile.
evidence_required: Mobile rendered verification confirms primary actions are visible or reachable through an expected mobile pattern.
Core mobile interactions exercise the primary action when behavior changed.

rules.title: Provide a mobile strategy for tables
rules.requirement: Tables provide a mobile strategy: priority columns, stacked rows, or detail drill-in.
evidence_required: Data table work records the selected mobile strategy: priority columns, stacked rows, or detail drill-in.
Mobile verification confirms the table strategy is usable without horizontal overflow.

rules.title: Collapse sidebars into mobile navigation
rules.requirement: Sidebars collapse into drawer or bottom navigation on mobile.
evidence_required: Navigation verification confirms desktop sidebar behavior and mobile drawer or bottom navigation behavior.
Mobile navigation remains reachable and does not block core content.

rules.title: Use mobile-appropriate sheets or drawers for modal-heavy flows
rules.requirement: Modal-heavy flows use sheets or drawers on mobile when appropriate.
evidence_required: Modal-heavy flow changes document whether mobile sheets or drawers are appropriate.
Mobile rendered verification confirms modal-heavy flows remain usable.
